list.name: shard
list.description: Sharded families only (subject-sharding v2). Empty and harmless in non-sharded installs.

panels.title: Key sync latency p50/p95/p99 (calculator, s)
panels.description: cdc_latency_seconds from the optional latency-calculator component (region XADD timestamp delta, rolling window; genuine seconds). Its job label is 'latency-calculator', distinct from the connect legs, so this panel scopes by namespace only. EMPTY if latencyCalculator.enabled=false.

panels.title: Sink sync latency mean (s)
panels.description: Mean writer-mint → sink-apply latency, per op. QUIRK of this pinned Connect build: cdc_sync_latency_seconds records RAW NANOSECONDS while its histogram buckets are labelled in seconds, so every real sample lands in +Inf and histogram_quantile over the _bucket series is unusable (returns +Inf). The mean is still recoverable from _sum/_count, and the trailing /1e9 converts ns → s. For true percentiles use the calculator panel above.

panels.title: Connection events: up / lost / failed
panels.description: Connect input/output connection lifecycle counters (no _total suffix on this build). 'up' events are normal on startup/reconnect; sustained 'lost'/'failed' means the Redis input or NATS output is flapping. On a sharded sink the output splits per shard child (path/label labels), aggregated away here by summing per job.

panels.title: Connection lost/failed (15m)
panels.description: Total input+output connection lost/failed events over 15m. Any nonzero value means a leg dropped its Redis or NATS connection in the window — investigate before it becomes redelivery backlog.

panels.title: Unprocessable messages (5m)
panels.description: cdc_unprocessable over 5m: messages the sink permanently rejected (decode_error / unknown_op). Nonzero means real data the pipeline cannot apply — see the by-reason panel below.

panels.title: Unprocessable rate by reason
panels.description: cdc_unprocessable broken out by reason (decode_error = payload could not be parsed; unknown_op = op field outside the create/update/delete/rename set). Each rejected message is counted here and NOT applied.

panels.title: JetStream publish failures (forward leg)
panels.description: Forward-leg publish-failure signal, which DIFFERS BY MODE. Non-sharded: cdc_forward_publish_failed{reason} fires in the output fallback's reject child (the publish errored and was NACKed for replay — no ack, no loss). SHARDED (subject-sharding v2): that fallback and counter are removed by design; failures retry IN PLACE and surface as output_error on the connect-source job instead (second series). One of the two series is expected to be flat depending on the render.

panels.title: Sink apply throughput (ops/s)
panels.description: cdc_apply rate per sink job and op (increments only after a successful region write). With multi-subject / sharded sink groups each group is its own Prometheus job, so `by (job)` breaks throughput out per group.

panels.title: Apply throughput by shard (ops/s)
panels.description: cdc_apply rate per shard (subject-sharding v2). Only sharded sink groups emit the shard label, so this row stays empty in non-sharded installs. Watch for balance: a hot shard saturates its serialized max_ack_pending=1 lane while others idle.

panels.title: Sync latency mean by shard (s)
panels.description: Mean writer-mint → sink-apply latency per shard. SAME QUIRK as the top-level mean panel: cdc_sync_latency_seconds records raw NANOSECONDS with seconds-labelled buckets, so histogram_quantile over the _bucket series is unusable (+Inf); the mean is recovered from _sum/_count and /1e9 converts ns → s. A single shard's mean climbing while others stay flat = that shard's serialized lane is saturated or blocked.

panels.title: Unprocessable by shard
panels.description: cdc_unprocessable per shard and reason (sharded groups only). Pinpoints which shard is receiving payloads it cannot decode or an op it does not recognise.

panels.title: Shard isolation lane (must stay 0)
panels.description: Sharding invariant signals (design v2 §11 A3): cross_shard_rename = a rename whose old/new keys resolve to different shards (INV-S7 — any nonzero value is a bug); unparseable_shard = a sharded-family key without a keyPattern id (key contract broken); sx applies = traffic through the isolation shard. All three route to sx so nothing is lost — but nonzero means the key contract or the shard function is broken. NOTE: v2 has NO direct out-of-order signal (no fence) — these are proxies. Drives the CDCShardIsolationLane alert.
